message:
fix(build_coord): detect rch and heavy cargo commands in chained shell expressions

command_uses_rch() and cargo_payload_tokens() previously only inspected
the first token sequence in a command string, missing cargo invocations
preceded by cd, env var assignments, or other shell commands joined by
&&, ||, ;, |, or &. A command like "cd /tmp && cargo test -p foo" would
not be recognized as a heavy cargo command and would bypass rch offload.

- Added next_command_segment_start() to walk past shell command
  separators (&&, ||, ;, |, &) to the next meaningful token
- Added is_shell_command_separator() predicate
- command_uses_rch() and cargo_payload_tokens() now iterate through all
  segments in a chained command pipeline
- Four new unit tests covering: cd && cargo test, env-prefixed cargo
  check, piped cargo, multi-segment with rch in later position

policy.rs (+25 lines):
- Two new policy-engine integration tests exercising the fix end-to-end:
  "cd /tmp && cargo test" without rch requires approval, and
  "cd /tmp && rch exec -- cargo check" is allowed

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
